author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_eeg): QA-native consecutive-quantisation model — Fisher p=5.8e-19
Replace KMeans-based combined-2 with a Theorem NT-compliant pipeline:
amplitude samples quantised to {1,...,9} by percentile, consecutive (b,e)
pairs classified by exact modular orbit conditions (Singularity b=e=9,
Satellite 3|b∧3|e), z-scored against interictal baseline.

Result on 12 held-out CHB-MIT patients: mean ΔR²=+0.275 (SE 0.074),
Fisher χ²=143.3, p=5.8×10⁻¹⁹, 11/12 positive.

Update paper.tex: new §7 QA-Native Held-Out Validation, updated abstract
and title to reflect the cleaner, stronger result.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Player3
content:
experiments(qa_eeg): Siena Scalp EEG replication — TREND_ONLY (pre-registered)
Pre-registered protocol (commit 85e58f94, 2026-06-02) applied to all 14 Siena
patients (PhysioNet siena-scalp-eeg/1.0.0, 512Hz, wall-clock annotations).

Primary result (threshold >=12 ictal windows, 9/14 patients):
  Mean ΔR² = +0.038 (SE 0.024), Fisher p = 0.970, 5/9 positive
  Verdict: TREND_ONLY — pre-registered endpoint (p < 0.05) not met

Post-hoc sensitivity (threshold >=6, declared, 13/14 patients):
  Mean ΔR² = +0.065 (SE 0.051), Fisher p = 0.974, 8/13 positive
  Also TREND_ONLY

Interpretation: inconclusive. Siena's short per-seizure recordings (60-120s)
vs CHB-MIT's multi-hour continuous monitoring creates a structural power
mismatch. Third-dataset test (TUEG) needed to distinguish dataset artifact
from genuine non-replication.

Changes:
  eeg_siena_combined2.py     — bug fixes (parser, f2_sat logic, dual threshold)
  eeg_siena_download.py      — urllib downloader with retry
  eeg_siena_combined2_results.json — full results
  paper.tex                  — new section with Siena results table + interpretation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
